behavior: Noisy, intelligent omnivore that forages on the ground and in trees; carries food to cache, holds seeds or nuts in its feet to peck them open, and may mimic hawk calls.
field_marks: Prominent blue crest that can be raised or lowered
Blue upperparts with pale gray to white underparts
Bold black facial markings and a black necklace across the throat
Strong, straight black bill and dark eyes
Blue wings and long, rounded tail barred with black, with white wingbars and tail corners
habitat: Forest edges, oak woods, mixed woodlands, woodlots, parks, and other tree-rich residential habitats.

sources.title: Blue Jay Identification — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
